long_description:
We ran the acadia platform upgrade using the CD job, however the upgrade failed due to the time out issue where the daemons start upgrading 
Need to monitor the upgrade until the cluster gets back healthy
short_description: To monitor the Ongoing Daemon upgrade in the FRA regional cluster mzonecb1
close_notes:
Current status:
[ceph: root@fra1-qz1-sr6-rk137-s06 /]# ceph orch upgrade status
There are no upgrades in progress currently.
Ceph health:
[ceph: root@fra1-qz1-sr6-rk137-s06 /]# ceph status
  cluster:
    id:     cc4306a2-fdfd-11ef-81d1-6cfe5499ebaa
    health: HEALTH_OK
 
  services:
    mon: 3 daemons, quorum fra1-qz1-sr6-rk137-s06,fra2-qz1-sr3-rk009-s06,fra3-qz1-sr3-rk064-s06 (age 9h)
    mgr: fra3-qz1-sr3-rk064-s06.yqffov(active, since 9h), standbys: fra2-qz1-sr3-rk009-s06.bhejni, fra1-qz1-sr6-rk137-s06.lrzofi
    mds: 3/3 daemons up, 3 hot standby
    osd: 504 osds: 504 up (since 29m), 504 in (since 3w)
 
  data:
    volumes: 1/1 healthy
    pools:   6 pools, 106560 pgs
    objects: 1.62M objects, 5.9 TiB
    usage:   18 TiB used, 1.4 PiB / 1.4 PiB avail
    pgs:     106560 active+clean
 
  io:
    client:   463 KiB/s rd, 415 KiB/s wr, 10 op/s rd, 10 op/s wr

Daemon upgrade has completed now, closing the Incident
Will not be attempting the platform re-run as the business hours started and since we have to run UpgradeAll job for this cluster, it would take care of the platform re-run after Os-upgrade.
